item.title: pageboy
item.description: The full source code and examples are available at: https://github.com/khtdr/pageboy pageboy Write your shell scripts in any combination of scripting languages. Example Script example.pb #!/usr/bin/env pageboy echo in Bash $PAGE php echo back to Bash #!/usr/bin/env php <?php echo "in PHP\n"; chmod +x ./example.pb ./example.pb produces the output: in Bash in PHP back to Bash tests ./run-tests.sh pageboy-v2.1.3 ./pageboy-test # runs as pageboy script ./pageboy-test -r <page> # runs requested page .

item.title: A zondicons package for react: react-zondicons
item.description: A dependency-free, small, fast, good-looking React SVG icon set. See: http://khtdr.com/react-zondicons/ for installation, usage, documentation, and examples.

item.title: Simple live-reloading Nodejs web server
item.description: The files in this tutorial can be found at: github.com/khtdr/live-reload-web-server Building your own live-reloading web server Follow along and build your own automatically-refreshing web server, as show here: This setup assumes you know and use Node and NPM You are editing HTML, CSS, and Javascript by hand, because you like it. You've had some of that hot-reloading goodness before, and you want it now, too.

item.title: Links
item.description: about me github/khtdr typescript advanced types utility types emacs doom emacs orgmode.org philosophy Diogenes of Sinope Stoic Ethics Stoic Logic /r/Stoicism javacript MDN web docs babel repl

item.title: Opts.js
item.description: You can find the source code and all examples at Github, github.com/khtdr/opts. Introduction to Opts.js Why choose opts.js over something bigger and better? Small : Under 10kb, uncompressed. Stable : Relatively unchanged since 2010. See changelog. Standalone : No package manager necessary, no compiling needed. See src/opts.js. Tested : Over 5 million downloads. See npm/opts. Quick-Start example The following example set up a custom "version" function, and opts in to the automatic help message.

item.title: A Tailwind-CSS development server
item.description: Simple quick way to get started learning and playing with Tailwind CSS Example output while running dev-server, shown here: Features No "transpiling" configuration is needed (or used). No build tools need to be set up. Tiny reactive web server: ~115 lines of code with lots of comments. Minimal dependencies: chalk for color, socket.io for server <-> browser communication,
